data.goal: Write four focused sensor-level test modules for the TestFailureSensor in-flight dedup mechanism shipped in commit 20baa93a1d. The mechanism lives in backend/core/ouroboros/governance/intake/sensors/test_failure_sensor.py — read that file and the existing sensor test at tests/governance/intake/sensors/test_test_failure_sensor.py before writing, so the new tests match the project's patterns (pytest-asyncio, stubbed router, explicit env-var setup). If helpful, read the nearby conftest patterns under tests/governance/intake/ for fixture conventions, but do not modify conftest or __init__ files — all new code must live in the four target test modules below. Each module covers one behavior in isolation: (A) test_test_failure_sensor_dedup.py — a second signal for the same target within the TTL window is suppressed with the expected 'already in-flight' log line; (B) test_test_failure_sensor_ttl.py — after TTL expiry the same target is re-admitted and a new op is created; (C) test_test_failure_sensor_isolation.py — concurrent signals for different target files are not cross-suppressed; (D) test_test_failure_sensor_marker_refresh.py — the in-flight marker is refreshed on successful enqueue. The TTL default is 60s; read it from the env var the sensor consults rather than hardcoding. Prefer tests that stub the router over tests that boot the full intake layer. [multi-file 4/4: tests/governance/intake/sensors/test_test_failure_sensor_marker_refresh.py]
